description: Streptomyces phage phiC31 bacterial attachment site attB; the 34 bp natural Streptomyces chromosomal target for the phiC31 large serine integrase.

references.title: In vitro site-specific integration of bacteriophage DNA catalyzed by a recombinase of the resolvase/invertase family

references.authors: Groth AC, Olivares EC, Thyagarajan B, Calos MP
references.title: A phage integrase directs efficient site-specific integration in human cells

label: phiC31_attB
note: phiC31 bacterial attachment site attB, the 34 bp natural target in the Streptomyces coelicolor chromosome recognized by the phiC31 large serine integrase (unidirectional; no host cofactors; cannot excise without an RDF). Central TT crossover dinucleotide; attB and attP share only a very short central core (~39% overall identity), the basis of the reaction directionality. In non-native hosts (human/other) phiC31 Int also recombines with degenerate genomic pseudo-attP sites; there is no single canonical pseudo-attB. Cognate phage site: phiC31_attP.

provenance.sequence_source: NC_003888.3 (Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2) chromosome), natural phiC31 attB, positions ~4178705..4178738 (+ strand), 100% identity over 34 bp
